name: Pana MIA Club Privacy Policy
_comment: SOURCE OF TRUTH for the privacy framework. The Privacy at a Glance grid (components/legal/PrivacyAtAGlance.tsx) and the prose tier list (app/legal/privacy/page.tsx) are both derived from this file via lib/legal/privacy-policy.ts -- do not hand-maintain category lists there. Machine-readable fields (name, data, source, purpose, retention) are snake_case and stable; the `display` object carries presentation copy only. `module` links a category to its legal terms module id in ../terms/namespaces.json.

display.prose: Account credentials and authentication tokens
display.blurb: Your login details — email, password, and the sign-in tokens that keep you logged in.

display.prose: Profile information (contact, address, descriptions, images, social links)
display.blurb: Your public directory listing: contact info, address, description, photos, and social links.

display.prose: Mentoring profile (expertise, languages, bio, hourly rate)
display.blurb: The mentor listing you fill out — your expertise, languages, short bio, and hourly rate.

display.prose: Session notes and mentoring session metadata
display.blurb: Notes you or your mentor write about a session, plus basic details like its topic.

display.prose: Social graph (follows, followers, likes)
display.blurb: Who you follow and the posts you've liked.

display.prose: RSVPs and event attendance records
display.blurb: The events you've said you're attending.

display.prose: Notification preferences
display.blurb: Your choices about which alerts and emails we send you.

display.prose: Intake form submissions
display.blurb: The answers you gave on the become-a-pana join form.

categories.name: nostr_identity
categories.label: Nostr Identity
categories.note: Your secret key (nsec) is generated in your browser and is never transmitted to or stored by Pana MIA Club. A bring-your-own key is verified with a signed proof of control rather than by uploading the key itself. Deleting your account removes the public key from our systems; it does not and cannot remove events you already published to Nostr.

display.prose: Nostr identity: your public key and whether it was generated for you or brought your own (your secret key is never sent to us)
display.blurb: Your public key for the community messaging network. Your secret key never leaves your device.

display.prose: Relay group membership records (which groups your public key belongs to, and when it joined)
display.blurb: Which community messaging groups your key belongs to, and when it joined.

categories.name: other_member_data
categories.label: Other Member Data
categories.note: A catch-all for smaller member-provided datasets: contact-form messages, newsletter signups (local list, distinct from the GoHighLevel CRM record), and the engagement/points activity ledger. All removed when the member deletes their account through the account-deletion flow.

display.prose: Other member data — contact-form messages, newsletter signups, and engagement/points activity
display.blurb: Smaller bits you've given us: contact-form messages, newsletter signups, and your activity points.

community_record.description: Anonymizable but not deletable after archive threshold

display.prose: Published articles (archive: 3 months after publication)
display.blurb: Articles you've published to the community.

display.prose: Article peer review comments (archive: follows article)
display.blurb: Feedback you leave reviewing another member's article before it's published.

categories.name: social_posts
categories.label: Social Posts
categories.note: Social timeline posts are always fully deleted on request regardless of age

display.prose: Social timeline posts (no archive threshold — always fully deleted)
display.blurb: Your posts, replies, and reposts on the community timeline.

display.prose: Event records (archive: after event completion)
display.blurb: Events you've created, including their details and description.

third_party_synced.description: Deletion initiated but subject to provider retention policies

display.prose: Stripe — email, payment method, transaction history (7-year legal hold)
display.blurb: Your donation and membership payments, handled by Stripe.

categories.name: transactional_email
categories.label: Transactional Email
categories.note: Sent through a native Cloudflare Workers binding with no contact list. There is no stored contact record to delete; Cloudflare may retain delivery logs under its own terms.

display.prose: Cloudflare Email Sending — your address at send time only. No contact list is kept, so there is nothing to delete; Cloudflare may retain delivery logs.
display.blurb: The address we send account emails to. We don't build a mailing list from it — it's used only at send time.

display.prose: GoHighLevel — contact ID, email, name, newsletter list membership
display.blurb: Your contact record in our newsletter/CRM system (GoHighLevel), including newsletter list membership.

display.prose: Cloudflare R2 — uploaded media files
display.blurb: Photos and files you've uploaded, stored on Cloudflare.

categories.name: oauth_identity
categories.label: OAuth Identity
categories.note: Received from the provider, not sent to them

display.prose: Google / Apple OAuth — email, name, profile image (received, not sent)
display.blurb: Basic info (email, name, picture) we receive when you sign in with Google or Apple. Nothing is sent back to them.

moderation_record.description: Safety records retained independently of the accounts they concern. Not deletable on request: a member cannot erase reports made about them, and a reporter cannot retroactively withdraw a record that moderators relied on.

display.prose: Relay abuse reports (NIP-56) — the reporter's and target's public keys, plus a snapshot of the reported content. Retained even after the accounts involved are deleted.
display.blurb: Reports members file about abusive messages on the relay. Kept even after an account leaves, so moderation records survive.

compliance_record.description: Records retained after account deletion to meet legal, audit, or operational obligations. Not user-deletable; kept under a legitimate-interest basis, not consent.

categories.name: consent_receipts
categories.label: Consent Receipts
categories.note: A record that you accepted a given policy version, kept so we can demonstrate consent existed. Retained when you delete your account rather than removed with it.

display.prose: Consent receipts — which policy version you accepted, when, and from what IP. Retained after account deletion as proof consent existed.
display.blurb: A record that you accepted a given version of our policies — proof the consent happened.

categories.name: deletion_audit
categories.label: Deletion Audit Log
categories.note: A record that an account deletion happened and what it did. It is itself the proof of deletion, so it necessarily outlives the account.

display.prose: Deletion audit log — a record that your account was deleted and what was removed. Kept as proof the deletion occurred.
display.blurb: A record that an account was deleted and what was removed — kept as proof the deletion took place.

categories.name: screenname_redirects
categories.label: Screenname Redirects
categories.note: Old screennames map to a 410 Gone response so federated servers stop resolving a departed member's handle. Retained so those redirects keep working.

display.prose: Screenname history — retained after deletion so federated servers get a stable 410 Gone for handles you no longer use.
display.blurb: Usernames you've used before, kept so links to a former handle still resolve on other servers.

temporaryData.description: Retained only as long as necessary, then automatically purged

display.prose: Mentoring session signaling and WebRTC connection metadata
display.blurb: Behind-the-scenes connection info that sets up a live mentoring call. Discarded when the session ends.

display.prose: Whiteboard state (purged 30 min after session ends)
display.blurb: The shared drawing board during a mentoring session. Erased 30 minutes after it ends.

display.prose: Real-time chat messages during mentoring sessions
display.blurb: Chat messages typed during a live mentoring session.

categories.name: session_streams
categories.label: Session Video/Audio
categories.note: Never recorded server-side unless explicitly enabled

display.prose: Session video/audio streams (never recorded server-side unless explicitly enabled)
display.blurb: The live video and audio of a session. Not recorded on our servers unless you turn recording on.

display.prose: OAuth tokens and transient authentication state
display.blurb: Temporary keys that keep your Google or Apple sign-in active.

display.prose: IP addresses and user-agent strings (90-day analytics window)
display.blurb: Your IP address and browser type, kept briefly (90 days) to understand site traffic.

categories.name: verification_tokens
categories.label: Verification Tokens
categories.note: Short-lived tokens with an expiry: sign-in magic links, email verification, and the token issued when changing your account email.

display.prose: Email verification, magic-link, and email-change tokens (expire per config)
display.blurb: Short-lived codes for signing in, verifying your email, or changing it. They expire quickly.

display.prose: Pending relay group join and leave requests (joins: until an admin acts; leaves: a 24-hour grace period)
display.blurb: Requests to join or leave a community group that are waiting on approval or a short grace period.

peerNetworkingData.description: Exchanged directly between participants, or propagated to servers we do not operate; not controlled by Pana MIA Club after transmission

in_the_wind.description: Published to an open, unbounded set of servers we do not operate. Deletion requests are advisory only -- a remote server MAY honor them and MAY ignore them. Neither Pana MIA Club nor anyone else can guarantee retraction once content has propagated.
in_the_wind.note: Categorically weaker than third_party_synced. There, a named provider receives a deletion request under a contract. Here there is no enumerable counterparty to ask.

categories.name: activitypub_federated_content
categories.label: Federated Social Content
categories.note: Remote servers may ignore a Delete activity

display.prose: Social posts, replies, likes, and follows federated via ActivityPub — a best-effort Delete is sent, but remote servers may ignore it
display.blurb: Posts and follows that leave our servers to reach Mastodon and similar networks. Once out, we can ask other servers to delete them but can't force it.

categories.name: nostr_published_events
categories.label: Nostr Events
categories.note: A Nostr event is signed by your key and stays cryptographically valid forever, so any relay may rehost it. NIP-09 delete requests are advisory: relays MAY honor them. There is no enumerable list of relays to ask. We can delete from our own relay; no one can delete from Nostr at large.

display.prose: Anything you publish to Nostr — group chat, profile metadata, RSVPs. We can remove it from our own relay; no one can remove it from Nostr at large.
display.blurb: Anything you publish to the Nostr network — group chats, profile info, RSVPs. We can remove it from our relay, but no one can remove it from Nostr everywhere.

participant_observed.description: Seen directly by the people you shared it with. We do not retain it; they may.

display.prose: Video and audio streams during mentoring sessions (WebRTC peer-to-peer)
display.blurb: The live video and audio of a mentoring call, sent directly between you and the other person, not through us.

display.prose: Whiteboard content visible to session participants
display.blurb: What you draw on the shared board, visible to the others in your session.

display.prose: Chat messages seen by the other participant before deletion
display.blurb: Session chat the other person already saw before it was deleted.

display.prose: Co-author content shared during article collaboration
display.blurb: Draft article content you share with a co-author while writing together.

display.prose: Profile information visible to other users
display.blurb: The parts of your profile other members can see.

display.prose: Event RSVP and attendance information visible to organizers and attendees
display.blurb: Your RSVP and attendance, visible to the event's organizers and other attendees.

display.prose: Information shared at in-person events (verbal, written, photos)
display.blurb: Anything you share face-to-face at an event — spoken, written, or photographed by others.

contentLicense.anonymizationNote: For CC-BY / CC-BY-SA, removing a member's name on archived content exercises the licensor's Section 3(a)(3) attribution-removal right. For CC0 there is no attribution requirement to remove; the platform simply stops displaying the attribution it showed voluntarily. The member's keep-or-remove choice applies to all three.
